channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Flames benefit from new players in 5-4 win against Hurricanes
item.description: Calgary Flames welcomed several new players in their lineup on Saturday, taking advantage of the departures of Nazem Kadri and MacKenzie Weegar. Joel Farabee and newly acquired Ryan Strome played key ...

item.title: Sen. Escudero Urges Private Sector to Adopt 4-Day Work Week to Ease Traffic Congestion and Fuel Price Impact
item.description: Senator Francis 'Chiz' Escudero called on private companies to adopt a four-day work week, following the government's lead, to help lessen the effects of rising fuel prices due to tensions in the Midd...

item.title: India vs New Zealand: T20 World Cup Final Preview and Key Match Insights
item.description: India host New Zealand in the T20 World Cup final at Ahmedabad's Narendra Modi Stadium, aiming to become the first nation to win a third men’s T20 title and to retain their crown. India reached the fi...

item.title: Yvette Cooper defends Starmer's cautious approach to Iran conflict, citing lessons from Iraq War
item.description: Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper has defended Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer's handling of the escalating conflict involving US strikes against Iran, emphasising that the UK is applying lessons learnt...

item.title: AI Leaders Discuss Potential Government Involvement in AI Development
item.description: CEOs from major AI companies, including Palantir and OpenAI, have publicly expressed concerns about the possibility of the U.S. government nationalizing AI technologies. Palantir's CEO warned that if ...

item.title: Recent Polls Show One Nation at 23–24% in Victoria While Major Parties See Mixed Support
item.description: Recent polling in Victoria shows One Nation polling at 23–24% of primary votes, while the lead between Labor and the Coalition varies depending on the poll. A Freshwater poll for The Herald Sun gave L...

item.title: Examining Palestinianism and the Concept of Zionophobia
item.description: In this opinion piece, Barbara Kay discusses the persistence of Palestinianism as an ideology in the West and its impact on Jewish communities. She argues that Palestinianism has shifted from a focus ...

item.title: KCMC to Open New Cardiac Unit in 2027 to Serve Northern Tanzania
item.description: Kilimanjaro Christian Medical Centre (KCMC) in Moshi, Kilimanjaro Region, is planning to open a dedicated cardiac treatment unit in 2027 with an investment of over Sh25 billion. Right now, specialised...

item.title: Prophet Kanyari Defends Bishop Kiengei After Hosting President Ruto at JCM Church
item.description: Bishop Muthee Kiengei has been at the centre of controversy after hosting President William Ruto at his JCM Church on February 22. This visit shocked many followers because Kiengei had previously crit...

item.title: Abdoul Razac Sankara to debut at lightweight in PFL Africa Season 2 in Pretoria
item.description: PFL Africa is gearing up for its Season 2 launch on 10 April 2026 at the SunBet Arena, Menlyn Maine, Pretoria. The card features several exciting match-ups, including last year’s bantamweight champion...

item.title: South Korea score first goal past Mackenzie Arnold in dominant match
item.description: In a recent soccer match, South Korea showcased their attacking prowess by scoring the opening goal against Mackenzie Arnold, the Australian goalkeeper. Mun Eunju was the player to put the ball past A...

item.title: Pre-feasibility Work on Arunachal's Siang Upper Project to Commence Shortly, Says Minister
item.description: The Arunachal Pradesh government is set to initiate pre-feasibility report (PFR) activities for the Siang Upper Multipurpose Project (SUMP) from March 20, 2026, according to Panchayati Raj and Rural D...

item.title: India Prepares for T20 World Cup Final Against New Zealand in Ahmedabad
item.description: Tens of thousands of Indian cricket fans gathered at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad for the T20 World Cup final, with millions more following the match on television. India faces New Zealand i...

item.title: NCDMB Commissions Modern Clinical Skills and Simulation Laboratory at Bayelsa Medical University
item.description: The Nigerian Content Development and Monitoring Board (NCDMB) has commissioned a new Clinical Skills and Simulation Laboratory for Bayelsa Medical University (BMU), Yenagoa. This top-class facility ge...

item.title: Charles Wachira Starts God’s Love International Church in Tacoma Following Departure from JCM
item.description: Former JCM lead preacher, Charles Wachira, has officially launched his own ministry, God’s Love International Church, in Tacoma, USA, just weeks after resigning from Bishop Muthee Kiengei’s church. Wa...

item.title: Philippine Women's Team Secures First Victory Over Iran in AFC Women's Asian Cup Group Stage
item.description: The Philippine women's national football team, affectionately known as the Filipinas, achieved a significant milestone in the 2026 AFC Women's Asian Cup by defeating Iran in their group stage encounte...

item.title: Government to Clarify Safety of Chemicals Found in Sanitary Products
item.description: The South African government has announced it will address growing public concern after a University of the Free State study detected hormone-disrupting chemicals in certain sanitary pads and panty li...

item.title: Ethan Sanders secures first NRL field goal and demonstrates match-winning potential
item.description: Ethan Sanders achieved a milestone in his NRL career during just his fifth game, kicking his first field goal to secure a 29-28 golden point victory against Manly at Brookvale. The Raiders had appeare...

item.title: The Constitutional and Economic Impact of Trump’s Military Strikes in Iran
item.description: Since January 2025, the Trump administration has launched over 600 military strikes in countries including Iran, Yemen, Nigeria, and Venezuela, with additional threats against Greenland, Colombia, and...

item.title: Marietha becomes one of the few female bodaboda riders in Dar es Salaam
item.description: Marietha Gurahe, hailing from Manyara Region, moved to Dar es Salaam with nothing but hope and determination to create a better life for herself. She faced the harsh reality of city life, including hi...
